title: roylea calycina
intro: Roylea calycina, also known as Himalayan rose or Royle's cinnamon, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Roylea calycina include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions due to its antimicrobial properties. Roylea calycina can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make a paste for topical application.
